meaning: adj.可消除的,可抹去的,使被忘却的
examples: Such devices are divided into blocks, called sectors, that are individually-erasable.
Such devices are divided into blocks, called sectors, that are individually - erasable.
He has attempted to etch-a-sketch an issue that inevitably leaves an un-erasable dark shadow on the screen.
Mr Cassagnes came up with the idea for a mechanical toy that creates erasable drawings by twisting two dials in the late 1950s, while working as an electrical technician.
